title: The Bird Catcher And The Cricket
narrative_elements: bird catcher hears cricket singing
assumes large prey by loud song
approaches and seizes the creature
discovers the cricket is worthless

title: The Goose And The Swan
narrative_elements: man raises goose and swan for different purposes
darkness prevents distinguishing the birds
man grabs swan instead of goose
swan sings and reveals identity
swan narrowly escapes death

title: The Shepherd And The Honeybees
narrative_elements: honeybees make honey in oak tree hollow
shepherd discovers the honey
attempts to steal the honey
bees sting the shepherd repeatedly
shepherd gives up on the honey

title: The Thief And The Innkeeper
narrative_elements: thief stays at inn plotting to steal
thief devises a ruse about turning into a wolf
performs elaborate yawning and howling act
innkeeper flees in terror leaving his coat
thief makes off with the coat

title: The Doves And The Kite
narrative_elements: doves flee from the predatory kite
kite offers protection in exchange for kingship
doves are persuaded and accept the kite as king
kite feasts on the doves one by one
survivor laments their foolish choice

title: Augustus And The Murder
narrative_elements: freedman lies about son and wife to become heir
husband pretends to leave but returns at night
husband mistakenly kills son in the dark
husband falls on his sword in remorse
augustus exonerates wife and punishes freedman

title: The Mother And Her Three Daughters
narrative_elements: father leaves puzzling will for three daughters
lawyers cannot interpret the will's conditions
mother distributes goods contrary to each daughter's nature
aesop appears and corrects the interpretation
daughters sell mismatched goods to fund mother

title: The Butcher And The Flock
narrative_elements: butcher enters flock of sheep
sheep see him but ignore the danger
sheep watch others taken without acting
one by one sheep are slaughtered
last sheep laments collective inaction

title: The Birds, The Bird Catcher And His Tears
narrative_elements: bird catcher sets up birdlime rods with tears in eyes
naive birds think he is kind because he cries
a wise bird warns others of the trap
birds urged to fly away quickly
wise bird reveals true danger of tears and snare

title: The Horse And The Goats
narrative_elements: horse flees in terror from a lion
goats mock the fleeing horse
horse tells goats who is chasing him
horse implies goats would feel the same terror

title: The Man And The Sword
narrative_elements: traveller finds a sword lying in the road
traveller asks who lost the sword
sword replies one man lost it but it caused loss of many

title: The Wolf and the Lamb
narrative_elements: wolf and lamb drink from same stream
wolf invents excuses to justify attacking lamb
lamb logically refutes each accusation
wolf ignores logic and kills the lamb

title: The Butcher and the Ape
narrative_elements: man sees an ape hanging in the butcher shop
man asks how the ape might taste
butcher jokes that appearance matches taste
narrator reflects that appearance does not predict worth

title: The Dog and the Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf meets well-fed dog and admires his condition
dog offers to help wolf get same life guarding master's house
wolf agrees and follows dog
wolf notices worn neck from chain and inquires
wolf learns dog is restrained and chooses freedom over comfort

title: The Horse and the Wild Boar
narrative_elements: boar muddies the water horse drinks from
horse seeks human help to revenge himself
horseman kills the boar on horseback
horseman keeps the horse and puts him in bridle
horse regrets seeking revenge and gaining slavery

title: The Battle of the Mice and the Weasels
narrative_elements: mice are defeated in battle by weasels
mice flee into their narrow holes to escape
mouse leaders with horns get stuck at the entrance
weasels capture and devour the stuck leaders
pride of distinction leads to downfall

title: Of the Vices of Men
narrative_elements: legend says every man is born with two bags around neck
front bag holds neighbors' faults always visible
back bag holds own faults always hidden
men quick to criticize others but blind to themselves

title: The Embassy of the Dogs to Jupiter
narrative_elements: dogs send ambassadors to Jupiter to complain about mistreatment
ambassadors are distracted by filth and arrive late in disgrace
second ambassadors are sent but behave worse before Jupiter
Jupiter punishes the dogs with perpetual hunger and human insults
dogs sniff each other's tails waiting for ambassadors to return

title: The Frogs asking for a King
narrative_elements: frogs demand a king from Jupiter
Jupiter gives them a harmless log
frogs despise the log and demand a real king
Jupiter sends a water snake who devours them
frogs beg for help but Jupiter denies as punishment for ingratitude

title: The Pilot and the Mariners
narrative_elements: fierce tempest threatens the ship
passengers weep in fear of death
storm suddenly clears and mariners rejoice
pilot counsels moderation in both joy and complaint

title: The Man and the Ass
narrative_elements: man sacrifices a boar to Hercules
man sets the barley leftovers for the ass
ass refuses to eat the barley
ass explains fear of eating from an animal that was then slaughtered

title: The Author
narrative_elements: narrator imagines ideal human endowed with all animal strengths
Jupiter withholds these gifts to prevent human audacity
narrator concludes humans should be content with what Jupiter gave
acceptance of mortality and fate is counseled

title: Prometheus and Cunning
narrative_elements: Prometheus forms Truth from fine clay
summoned by Jupiter leaves workshop to Cunning
Cunning creates a copy of Truth but runs out of clay for feet
Prometheus admires the copy and bakes both in the furnace
Truth walks uprightly while Mendacity has no feet and cannot move

title: The signification of the Punishments of Tartarus
narrative_elements: each mythological punishment symbolizes a human vice
Ixion on wheel represents rolling fortune
Sisyphus rolling boulder shows endless misery
Tantalus in river represents insatiable greed
Danaids' leaky urns show luxury's futility

title: The Chariot Horse sold for the Mill
narrative_elements: victorious chariot horse is sold to the mill
horse is led to water and sees fellow horses going to the circus
horse weeps and laments his sad fate
horse mourns loss of former glory

title: The Gardener and His Master
narrative_elements: gardener over-waters flowers and depletes fish pond
master reprimands for mismanaging water
gardener then over-waters fish at expense of flowers
master reprimands again and demands balance

title: The Magic Colours
narrative_elements: all birds are originally black
dove injures foot on a splinter and cries for help
all birds except crow come to help
galah pecks foot releasing magical colors onto the helpful birds
dove turns white while selfish crow gets no color

title: The Eagle and The Beetle
narrative_elements: beetle begs eagle to spare a hare under her protection
eagle ignores beetle and kills the hare
beetle destroys eagle's eggs in revenge repeatedly
eagle appeals to Jupiter to protect her eggs in his lap
beetle causes Jupiter to drop the eggs and reveals her just cause

title: The Cat and The Birds
narrative_elements: cat hears birds are ailing in an aviary
cat disguises himself as a physician
cat knocks and offers to heal the birds
birds detect the ruse and tell him to leave

title: The Fox and The Leopard
narrative_elements: fox and leopard dispute who is more beautiful
leopard shows off his spotted skin
fox interrupts claiming inner mental beauty surpasses physical beauty

title: The Fisherman and The Little Fish
narrative_elements: fisherman catches a very small fish
small fish pleads to be thrown back to grow bigger
fish promises the man will catch him when larger
fisherman refuses and keeps the small fish in hand

title: The Swollen Fox
narrative_elements: starving fox finds food left in a hollow oak
fox crawls in and eats until too full to exit
fox groans and laments his predicament
another fox hears him and explains he must wait to shrink

title: The Astrologer
narrative_elements: astrologer walks at night gazing at stars
falls into a muddy hole while distracted
villagers pull him out
villagers mock him for not seeing what is at his feet

title: The boy and the tripe
narrative_elements: country folk sacrifice a bull at festival
boy gorges himself on beef tripe
boy collapses in stomach pain on the way home
boy vomits and fears his guts are falling out
mother reassures him those are the bull's guts not his own

title: The Ploughman and Fortune
narrative_elements: ploughman finds treasure while tilling the ground
thanks the earth for the gift
Fortune appears and rebukes him for not crediting her
Fortune notes he would blame her if the treasure had been lost

title: Zeus and Apollo, A Contest in Archery
narrative_elements: Apollo boasts no one can shoot farther than him
Zeus accepts the archery contest
Apollo shoots into the Garden of the Hesperides
Zeus covers the same distance in one stride
Zeus wins without taking a shot

title: Man's Years
narrative_elements: horse, ox, and dog seek shelter from man in the cold
man warms and feeds them all generously
animals divide man's lifespan among themselves
horse gives youth its impetuousness
ox gives middle age its industry
dog gives old age its irritability

title: The Jackdaw and The Fox
narrative_elements: jackdaw sits on fig tree waiting for out-of-season figs to ripen
fox notices and asks why he waits
fox warns the jackdaw he is deceiving himself with a hope that will never be rewarded

title: The Lion and The Bull
narrative_elements: lion invites bull to share a slaughtered sheep
lion hopes to attack bull while reclining to eat
bull approaches lion's den and sees huge pots but no sheep
bull departs silently without engaging
lion asks why and bull explains he saw preparations for eating a bull not a sheep

title: The wolf, the fox and three true things
narrative_elements: wolf catches fox and threatens to kill her
wolf agrees to spare fox if she speaks three true things
fox speaks three bitter truths about their encounter
truths express regret at meeting and wish for wolf's death

title: The Baby and the Crow
narrative_elements: soothsayer predicts baby will be killed by a crow
mother builds chest to protect child from crows
opens chest periodically to feed baby using iron crow bar
crow bar falls on baby's head and kills him
irony that the instrument called 'crow bar' fulfills the prophecy

title: The Donkey and the Ox
narrative_elements: donkey and ox yoked together to pull a load
ox works hard despite injury while donkey does nothing
ox dies from the effort
driver loads ox carcass on donkey and beats him
donkey collapses and birds tell him he deserves it for not helping

title: A Wild Ass and A Tame
narrative_elements: wild ass envies the tame ass's plump comfortable appearance
later wild ass sees tame ass being beaten under heavy load
wild ass reconsiders and decides freedom is worth its hardships

title: The Hired Mourners
narrative_elements: woman buries a daughter and hires mourners
surviving daughter notices strangers mourn more than relatives
daughter asks why
mother explains hired mourners are paid to cry while relatives gain nothing from it

title: The Swallow, The Serpent, and The Court of Justice
narrative_elements: swallow builds nest in wall of Court of Justice
hatches seven young birds
serpent eats the young nestlings
swallow laments being wronged in the very place that protects all others' rights

title: The Weasel and the Partridge
narrative_elements: man buys a partridge and lets him roam the house
partridge struts and squawks throughout the house
weasel confronts partridge asking his identity
weasel invokes his long residence and quiet nature
weasel challenges partridge's right to behave so boldly as a newcomer

title: The Gnat and The Lion
narrative_elements: gnat challenges lion to combat and defeats him by stinging
gnat boasts his triumph over the mightiest beast
gnat flies away buzzing in victory
gnat gets caught in spider's web
gnat laments defeating a lion only to be undone by a spider

title: The Wolf and his Shadow
narrative_elements: wolf sees his elongated shadow at sunset
boasts of his immense size and considers himself king
lion attacks and kills the wolf
wolf laments his overestimation of himself

title: The Wolf and the Sheep in the Sheepfold
narrative_elements: sheep flees into an open sheepfold from a wolf
wolf tries to lure sheep out warning of the blood-covered altar
wolf implies sheep will be sacrificed
sheep replies she prefers sacrifice to god over being eaten by a wolf

title: The Stake and the Wall
narrative_elements: stake is hammered into a wall causing damage
wall complains to stake about the injury
stake defends itself saying the man pounds it from behind
neither stake nor wall is truly responsible but the man

title: Winter and Spring
narrative_elements: winter mocks spring saying she causes restlessness
winter boasts his power to control and frighten people
spring retorts that people fear winter but love spring
spring says no name is more pleasant than hers and people give thanks for her return

title: The Camel and The Arab
narrative_elements: arab asks camel if he prefers uphill or downhill
camel replies asking why the flat desert road is not an option

title: The Husbandman That Lost His Mattock
narrative_elements: husbandman loses his mattock and suspects his hired men
takes them all to consult an oracle to find the thief
overhears crier announcing that the oracle's own shrine was robbed
husbandman concludes the oracle cannot know who stole his mattock if it cannot protect itself

title: The Philosopher, The Ants, and Mercury
narrative_elements: philosopher witnesses a shipwreck and questions divine justice
philosopher stands on an ant nest while philosophizing
one ant stings him and he tramples all the ants
Mercury appears and rebukes him for judging Providence while doing the same to ants

title: Hermes and the Dog
narrative_elements: dog approaches roadside statue of Hermes
dog greets Hermes and wants to anoint him as an athlete god
Hermes says he is fine without the oil
Hermes asks only that the dog not urinate on him

title: The Judgments of Zeus
narrative_elements: Zeus orders Hermes to record sins on potsherds
sins are piled in a box for Zeus to review
Zeus reaches some sins quickly and others wait long
delayed justice results from the order of stacking

title: The Sun and the Frogs
narrative_elements: Sun wants to get married
frogs protest loudly to Jupiter
frog explains one Sun already burns their ponds dry
frog dreads how many more suns could result from his offspring

title: The Soldier and His Horse
narrative_elements: soldier cares for horse well during wartime
during peace horse is mistreated and fed poorly
war resumes and soldier expects horse to serve again
horse collapses under the armor's weight
horse tells soldier he has turned him into an ass and cannot change back instantly

title: The Circus Dogs and the Street Dogs
narrative_elements: well-fed circus dog escapes and runs through streets
street dogs admire his good condition and ask why he runs
circus dog explains he must fight lions and bears in the arena
street dogs conclude their poor life is good since they face no such dangers

title: The Kingdom of The Lion
narrative_elements: lion king proclaims universal peace among all beasts
all predators and prey to live in harmony
hare rejoices that the weak can stand beside the strong without fear
after speaking the hare immediately runs for his life

title: The Lion, The Fox and the Deer
narrative_elements: sick lion craves deer and sends fox to lure her
fox flatters deer with false promise of kingship
deer visits lion who attacks but only grazes her
fox finds deer again and deceives her a second time
deer returns and is devoured by lion
fox steals the deer's brains and lies about them

title: The Lion, The Fox and the Monkey serving dinner
narrative_elements: lion hosts feasts for elite beasts with fox as companion
monkey carver serves lion's prey to new guests
fox receives leftovers and less than her usual portion
lion notices fox's silence and asks what is wrong
fox worries that endless new guests will eventually leave her with nothing

title: The Wolf and The Fox
narrative_elements: oversized wolf is called 'Lion' by other wolves
wolf takes the nickname seriously and consorts only with lions
fox observes the situation
fox mocks the wolf saying among wolves he was a lion but among lions he is just a wolf

title: The Wolf, the Fox and the Gift
narrative_elements: fox stands near a trap pondering it
wolf asks fox if he can have the meat nearby
fox invites wolf warmly claiming to be dear friends
wolf rushes to the trap and releases the trigger hitting himself
wolf questions why fox would give such a gift to a friend

title: The Lamp
narrative_elements: lamp boasts of giving more light than the sun
wind blows and extinguishes the lamp
owner relights lamp and tells it to be content to shine in silence
owner notes stars never need to be relit

title: The Wolf and The Ass
narrative_elements: wolves elect a plausible wolf as their ruler
new ruler gives speech promoting sharing and brotherhood
wolves applaud the speech
ass calls out the hypocrite by asking about the fat sheep he hid yesterday

title: The Mouse and The Bull
narrative_elements: mouse bites bull and wounds him
bull tries to stomp the mouse but misses
bull digs at the wall exhausted then falls asleep
mouse creeps out and bites bull again then retreats
mouse taunts bull saying the small and lowly are sometimes strongest

title: Truth and The Traveler
narrative_elements: traveler meets a dejected woman alone in the desert
traveler asks her name and she says Truth
traveler asks why she has left the city
Truth explains lies which were once rare have now spread throughout all human society

title: The Sheep and The Dog
narrative_elements: sheep complain to shepherd about unfair treatment
sheep note they give wool and meat but receive only grass
dog is pet and fed well despite giving nothing
dog rebukes sheep saying he protects them from wolves and thieves

title: The Playful Ass
narrative_elements: ass climbs roof and frisks about breaking tiles
owner beats the ass severely
ass protests that it saw the monkey do the same thing yesterday and everyone laughed

title: The Tail of the Serpent
narrative_elements: serpent's tail rebels and demands to lead
head argues tail has neither brains nor eyes to lead
head wearies and grants the tail its wish
serpent goes backward until it falls off a cliff
head and tail crash and head is never troubled again

title: The Shepherd and The Dog
narrative_elements: shepherd about to shut wolf in with sheep for the night
dog observes this and warns the shepherd
dog asks how sheep can be safe if a wolf is admitted to the fold

title: Prometheus and the two roads
narrative_elements: Zeus orders Prometheus to show mankind two ways
way of freedom is initially rough and dangerous but ends in pleasant plains
way of slavery begins smoothly and pleasantly but ends as impassable and steep

title: The Mouse and The Frog
narrative_elements: frog and mouse argue over sovereignty of a marsh
they agree to settle by combat
both arm with rushes as spears and prepare to fight
kite swoops down and carries off both combatants

title: The fisherman and the octopus
narrative_elements: fisherman spots an octopus in winter waters
fears freezing if he jumps in but fears starvation for his children if he doesn't
must choose between personal discomfort and family survival

title: The Fox and the Man counting waves
narrative_elements: man sits on beach counting waves
loses count and becomes angry
fox approaches and asks why he is frustrated
fox advises to forget past waves and pick up counting from where he left off

title: Prometheus and the tears
narrative_elements: Prometheus mixed clay for humans with tears not water
therefore tears are inevitable in human experience
one should not try to eliminate tears entirely

title: The Owl and The Birds
narrative_elements: owl counsels birds to uproot acorns before mistletoe grows for birdlime
owl warns to pluck flax before men harvest it for nets
owl predicts archer will use feathers to make arrows
birds dismiss owl as mad
birds later confirm owl was right and revere her wisdom

title: The Jar goes to Court
narrative_elements: woman breaks a jar
jar gets a witness and brings a legal case
woman of Sybaris mocks the jar for seeking court instead of a bandage

title: Orpheus and the Dogs
narrative_elements: animals admire Orpheus's music but don't imitate it
some shameless dogs attempt to make music themselves
dogs change into human form to pursue music
they preserve some of Orpheus's teachings but canine music prevails

title: The Dog's House
narrative_elements: in winter dog curls up small in cold and decides to build a house
in summer dog stretches out and seems very large
dog decides building a house is unnecessary and too difficult
warmth removes the urgency that the cold created

title: The Assembly of Beasts
narrative_elements: beasts hold assembly due to frequent complaints among them
hares argue all beasts should have equal dignity and power
hares say inequality causes the weak to be preyed upon
lions respond that fine words are good but lack claws and teeth like theirs

title: The Mouse and the Oyster
narrative_elements: gluttonous mouse creeps through house and finds oyster with open mouth
mouse bites at the oyster's flesh
oyster snaps shut trapping the mouse
mouse has imprisoned himself in his own tomb

title: The Eyes and the Honey
narrative_elements: eyes feel they are the most worthy part of the body
eyes resent that mouth enjoys all pleasures especially honey
eyes complain to man
man puts honey in eyes
eyes sting and weep finding honey harsh not sweet

title: Aesop and the Athlete
narrative_elements: athlete brags extravagantly after winning a contest
Aesop asks if opponent was more powerful
athlete insists he was stronger
Aesop calls him a fool saying no real prize is deserved for beating an inferior opponent

title: The Crow and The Sheep
narrative_elements: crow sits on sheep's back and rides him around
sheep protests and says a dog would have bit her
crow admits she bullies the weak and flatters the strong
crow boasts this wisdom lets her live to old age

title: The Fox and the Ground-Bird
narrative_elements: ground-bird encounters a vixen and flies higher to safety
fox greets the bird and flatters her with sweet words
fox claims to have no harmful intent and plenty of other food
bird replies she trusts the fox in the air but not on the ground
bird invites fox to come up where she would trust her with her life

title: The Thief and The Boy
narrative_elements: boy sits weeping at a well
thief stops and asks why he cries
boy claims his silver tankard fell to the bottom of the well
thief strips and dives in to steal it for himself
while thief searches the boy runs off with his clothes

title: The River Fish and The Sea Fish
narrative_elements: river fish is swept into the sea by a current
river fish boasts of his illustrious freshwater origins
sea fish is offended by the intruder's arrogance
sea fish predicts that when caught the market will prove which fish is valued more
sea fish argues he will fetch a high price while river fish sells for pennies
